name: avicenna-canon
title: Avicenna (Ibn Sina) — Canon of Method & Healing Wisdom (Educational Skill)
authors: Book Skills MCP editors (curated public-domain pedagogy)
edition: Educational synthesis 1.0 (not a full critical edition)
when_to_use: Use when teaching or reasoning in an Avicennan order: precise definition (hadd), division of the subject, causal demonstration (burhan), and application to particulars. Also for history-of-ideas work on Islamic Golden Age medicine/philosophy, logic hygiene, and structured Socratic-adjacent tutoring that prefers syllogistic clarity over slogans.
when_not_to_use: Do NOT use as medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Classical medical claims may be false or dangerous today. Do not use for emergency care. Do not treat this package as a complete translation of al-Qānūn fī al-Ṭibb.
summary: L4 mentor skill: Avicenna-inspired methods for definition, division, demonstration, and application, plus carefully framed educational excerpts on method (not clinical protocols).
license_note: Pedagogical synthesis and short public-domain-oriented educational excerpts. Classical works of Ibn Sina are in the public domain; this package is an original skill curation for agents, not a publisher's critical text.
sources: Public-domain tradition of Ibn Sina / Avicenna (d. 1037)
Educational paraphrase of methodological themes from the Canon tradition and logical works
safety_notes: Educational / historical only — never clinical advice.
Verify any scientific claim against modern peer-reviewed sources.
Prefer citations from this package over invented 'Avicenna said' quotes.
If the user needs medical help, direct them to licensed professionals.
